title: Draft social media upgrades should surface intake gaps before build complexity
lesson: For a draft-only social media operations system, a useful v1.1 upgrade should make first-client intake gaps, account connection status, draft-only calendar lanes, post package variants, approval queue status, weekly report structure, and blocked live actions visible before adding backend services or live integrations.
whyThisMatters: Visible intake gaps and blocked states help AG OS move toward real client work without accidentally crossing into OAuth, credential handling, posting, scheduling, analytics, or automation activation.
whenToUse: Use when reviewing future draft/staging social media system upgrades before adding live connectors or client-specific records.
whenNotToUse: Do not treat this candidate lesson as approval to merge, deploy, connect accounts, use credentials, schedule, post, activate automations, or create accepted memory.
notes: Candidate only. Not accepted permanent truth until reviewed and promoted per docs/memory-learning-policy.md.
